title: White House Directed Patel to Oversee Investigation Involving Times Reporting
summary: The F.B.I. director spent about eight hours at the White House Friday focused on the effort, which led to the subpoenaing of several Times reporters who wrote about the security of Air Force One.

title: How Marco Rubio Is Running Venezuela From Afar
summary: The secretary of state effectively controls Venezuela’s finances, the distribution of its natural resources and its government. His grip on the country is a vivid manifestation of American power in the Trump era.

title: Trump’s Posts on Singing Somali Schoolchildren Stir Anger in Minnesota
summary: The state’s large Muslim and Somali communities expressed indignation after the president reposted a video of a kindergarten promotion ceremony, including comments noting the girls were in hijabs.

title: Whistle-Blowers Accuse Kennedy Center of Contracting Flaws Under Trump
summary: Documents submitted to Congress detailed concerns about competitive bidding processes and a White House order to tear up new bathroom tile because of its color.

title: Videos Shed Light on Pursuit Before ICE Killing in Houston
summary: Surveillance footage obtained by The Times shows ICE agents driving aggressively in unmarked vehicles, but the moment of the fatal shooting of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo remains murky.

title: At Vigil, Sons of Man Killed by ICE in Houston Call for Accountability
summary: The sons of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo recalled their father’s love of soccer and said they would “continue to keep fighting for him.”

title: What We Know About the ICE Shooting of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo
summary: Mr. Araujo was a father, a husband and a business owner who had moved to the United States 35 years ago from Mexico.

title: Black Hawk Helicopters Rescue Hundreds From Summer Camp During Floods
summary: The National Guard evacuated campers after flooding in Missouri that reminded some families of the devastating floods at Camp Mystic in central Texas last year.

title: Mick Jagger Knows He May Have Played His Last Rolling Stones Show
summary: The legendary rock star, now 82, on how fame, touring and aging have changed him.

title: We’re Living in a Tick Nightmare. It’s Time to Go to War.
summary: With ticks, we’ve barely begun to fight back. The longer we wait, the more territory they will claim across both our geographic and psychic maps.

title: Arsenal Legend Bob Wilson on the Loneliest Role in Soccer
summary: For the Arsenal legend Bob Wilson, goalies must have “a desperate sense of courage.”

title: Moscow Still Has Art and Culture. Just Don’t Say ‘Ukraine.’
summary: An eerie limbo prevails at small art shows, independent theaters and private political clubs, where the war is the elephant in the room.
